title: Heat of Reaction and Calorimetry
content: The heat of reaction (ΔH_rxn) is the enthalpy change when stoichiometric amounts of reactants fully convert to products at constant pressure. Measured via solution calorimetry: q = mcΔT, where m is solution mass, c is specific heat capacity, and ΔT is the temperature change. Exothermic reactions (ΔH<0) release heat; endothermic (ΔH>0) absorb it. Heat transfer rate dQ/dt drives thermal safety constraints. Adiabatic calorimetry eliminates heat loss to surroundings for precise measurement.
